🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Brodie Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside
*County:* Moray
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 57.5984505, -3.7089562
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brodie_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Brodie%20Castle%20Moray%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZP9x525NA__rLZzkV51hps8YNkNi1E_mpypQtHgbya4rw9QqJLFeHbTB5f67D4OWm3Cr2EgT_z2GRSj4pe72srN1YWP-v6wQs9hnRTx4Fg8GHI_zUy-nQFuHVEV8R6VvsC5v4cME1s_iHAVsU8=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Brodie Castle is a well-preserved Z-plan tower house located north of Brodie about 3.5 miles west of Forres, in Moray, Scotland. It is a Category A listed building, and the grounds are included in the Inventory of Gardens and Designed Landscapes in Scotland.

_Listing 7 of 10_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Brechin Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside
*County:* Angus
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century onwards
*Coords:* 56.72916667, -2.65888889
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brechin_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Brechin%20Castle%20Angus%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b2/Brechin_Castle_and_Drive_-_geograph.org.uk_-_4029856.jpg/330px-Brechin_Castle_and_Drive_-_geograph.org.uk_-_4029856.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:*  google places link https://maps.app.goo.gl/Qv1w4ySfTLaU632E6 , add as source  https://dalhousieestates.co.uk/

*Description:*
Brechin Castle is a castle in Brechin, Angus, Scotland. The castle was constructed in stone during the 13th century. Most of the current building dates to the early 18th century, when extensive reconstruction was carried out by architect Alexander Edward for James Maule, 4th Earl of Panmure, between approximately 1696 and 1709. The castle is a 37,748 square feet (3,506.9 m2) Category A listed building and the grounds are included in the Inventory of Gardens and Designed Landscapes in Scotland.

_Listing 9 of 10_

The castle is a 37,748 square feet (3,506.9 m2) Category A listed building and the grounds are included in the Inventory of Gardens and Designed Landscapes in Scotland.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* New Slains Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside
*County:* Aberdeenshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 57.4153354, -1.8322935
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Slains_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/New%20Slains%20Castle%20Aberdeenshire%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a8/New_Slains_Castle_-_geograph.org.uk_-_8035498.jpg/330px-New_Slains_Castle_-_geograph.org.uk_-_8035498.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Slains Castle, also known as New Slains Castle to distinguish it from the nearby Old Slains Castle, is a ruined castle in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. It overlooks the North Sea from its cliff-top site one kilometre east of Cruden Bay.

_Listing 10 of 10_

listing.name: New Slains Castle
listing.desc: Slains Castle, also known as New Slains Castle to distinguish it from the nearby Old Slains Castle, is a ruined castle in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. It overlooks the North Sea from its cliff-top site one kilometre east of Cruden Bay.
